source: Incus Server - 6 Profiles: Creating a macvlan profile and assigning it to containers

questions.q: What is special about the default profile?
options: You cannot remove or modify it, but you use it as the basis for new profiles
questions.why: The default profile is fixed; you build additional profiles alongside it.

questions.q: What does macvlan let you do?
options: Create many interfaces with different Layer 2 (MAC) addresses, so containers get LAN/WAN IPs
questions.why: macvlan gives each container its own L2 address to reach your LAN or WAN.
incus profile create <name> makes an empty profile.

questions.q: How do you find the parent interface (the one with the LAN IP) for the profile?
options: Run `ip addr` and look for the interface holding the LAN IP (e.g. enp3s0)
questions.why: ip addr shows which interface (e.g. enp3s0) carries the 192.168.1.0/24 address.

questions.q: Which command adds the macvlan NIC device to the profile?
options: incus profile device add macvlan eth0 nic nictype=macvlan parent=enp3s0
questions.why: profile device add ... nictype=macvlan parent=<iface> wires the profile to the parent interface.

questions.q: How do you apply BOTH the default and macvlan profiles to a container?
questions.why: profile assign takes a comma-separated list; default,macvlan keeps default and adds macvlan.
